title: Review: Zcash wallet key management + payment data model
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two issues stand out: (1) the hidden mnemonic entry will generate immediate user reports; (2) the PaymentSession datetime type is a latent bug that will hit silently during nie-neg. The --network string validation is a papercut. Key separation check gap is a documentation promise vs. implementation mismatch that will confuse future contributors.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) bip39 v2.x API — I verified generate_in→from_entropy_in at runtime but did not check every method guarantee (e.g., to_seed always 64 bytes, word_iter vs. to_string().split behavior under edge cases); (2) age crate scrypt parameters — I assumed the age library's default scrypt work factor is appropriate; I did not audit the actual N/r/p used. Would raise confidence if: ran wallet_init with a mnemonic containing repeated words and verified the display numbering (the P0 bug), and read the bip39 crate source to confirm to_string() always uses ASCII space as separator.
